title: Azure Logic Apps - SharePoint Connector Conventions
applies_to: Logic Apps workflows that use the SharePoint Online connector for list or file operations

confirmed_facts.fact: The SharePoint Online connector exposes actions such as Create item, Update item, Delete item, Get items, Create file, Update file, Delete file, Get file content, and Get files (properties only).
The SharePoint Online connector is a managed connector surface rather than a built-in Logic Apps operation.
Many Logic Apps connectors require creating a separate connection resource that grants access to the target service.

implementation_notes: Use the SharePoint connector when designer simplicity and common list or file actions matter more than low-level Graph control.
Use Graph instead of the connector when you need richer SharePoint site discovery, drive semantics, or a single consistent Microsoft Graph auth and API model across the workflow.
When generating Logic Apps solutions, specify whether the target is a SharePoint list, a document library file, or metadata-only file properties because the connector uses different actions for each.
Treat the SharePoint connection as a managed resource dependency in deployment planning, not just a per-action setting.
caveats: Connector behavior and available auth modes can differ from raw Microsoft Graph behavior.
Using the connector and Graph in the same workflow is possible, but it increases operational complexity unless there is a clear reason.
Managed connector networking and connection behavior differ from built-in operations in Standard logic apps.
